text:
King's College Hospital 

Department of Neurosciences 

Clinic Date: 30 November 2021 

Dr Priya Menon
Consultant Neurologist  
Riverside Health Centre  
Riverside Health Centre, 14 Brook Street, London SE1 3AB  

Dear Dr Priya Menon
Consultant Neurologist,  

Daniel Carter, DOB: 14-03-1986, Hospital No: H732915 NHS No. 9075614421  
22 Oakfield Close, London, N7 5PL

Thank you for asking me to review your patient. He attended with his partner and provided a clear account of recent events. He has been using smartphone alarms linked to NFC tags placed by his pill box to prompt morning and evening doses after previously missing doses; he reports this has improved consistency over the past month.

Regarding the paroxysmal events, he and his partner describe brief spells characterised by marked pallor and queasiness with a sense of internal “rush,” sometimes accompanied by a need to sit down. These autonomic episodes are occurring approximately once every two weeks, with occasional clusters under stress, and are concerning for a seizure-related phenomenon. There have been no witnessed convulsions, tongue biting, or injuries reported. Post-event fatigue lasts 10–20 minutes without clear confusion. No specific triggers are identified beyond poor sleep.

Medications: He remains on his current antiseizure regimen without recent dose changes. He denies adverse effects beyond mild daytime tiredness. Adherence has improved with the phone/NFC reminder system; he estimates zero missed doses in the past four weeks compared with 2–3 missed evening doses per month previously.

Examination today was unremarkable. Cardiovascular observations were stable and there were no focal neurological deficits. ECG from the GP last month was normal by report. He does not drive.

Plan: 
- Continue current regimen and adherence supports (smartphone alarms and NFC tagging).
- Request ambulatory EEG and sleep-deprived EEG to better characterise the events. 
- Baseline bloods (FBC, U&Es, LFTs, vitamin D, ASM levels where appropriate). 
- Provide seizure first-aid guidance to partner; safety advice reinforced, particularly around water and heights. 
- We will review in 3 months or sooner if events escalate or evolve.

I have discussed the above with him and his partner; they are in agreement with the plan.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: These autonomic episodes are occurring approximately once every two weeks, with occasional clusters under stress

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter describes ongoing paroxysmal/autonomic episodes concerning for seizures. It gives an explicit frequency: "approximately once every two weeks" and mentions "occasional clusters under stress" without quantifying cluster frequency or events per cluster. Per the rules, use the highest quantified frequency; the cluster detail is insufficient to adjust. Normalize "once every two weeks" to "1 per 2 week."
seizure_frequency_number: These autonomic episodes are occurring approximately once every two weeks, with occasional clusters under stress

flaws.description: Gold (1 per 2 week) disagrees with Reference (unknown)
